context_pack.industry_context: The home gardening industry has seen growth in recent years, especially during the COVID-19 pandemic, as more people take up gardening as a hobby. There is an increasing trend towards organic and sustainable gardening practices.

terminology_glossary.Germination: The process by which a seed develops into a new plant.
terminology_glossary.Loamy soil: A type of soil that is a mix of sand, silt, and clay, known for its fertility and good drainage.
terminology_glossary.Vining plant: A plant that grows by extending stems or runners.
terminology_glossary.Pollination: The transfer of pollen from male to female parts of flowers, necessary for fruit production.

typical_workflows.traditional_process: To grow watermelon in Southern California, gardeners typically: 1) Select a sunny location with well-drained, loamy soil, 2) Start seeds indoors or plant directly after the last frost, 3) Water regularly, keeping soil moist but not waterlogged, 4) Apply mulch to retain moisture and control weeds, 5) Ensure pollination by attracting bees or manually transferring pollen.
